title: ddl: drop multiple partitions for a table with global index causes ddl panic
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)
```sql
use test;
drop table if exists test_global;
create table test_global ( a int, b int, c int)
	partition by range( a ) (
		partition p1 values less than (10),
		partition p2 values less than (20),
		partition p3 values less than (30)
	);
Alter Table test_global Add Unique Index idx_b (b);
INSERT INTO test_global VALUES (1, 1, 1), (2, 2, 2), (11, 3, 3), (12, 4, 4), (21, 21, 21), (29, 29, 29);
alter table test_global drop partition p1, p2;  -- ddl panic here
```
<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)
execute success
### 3. What did you see instead (Required)
ddl panic
![image](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/assets/20724552/fca44fdb-99d7-49b7-8a32-efa9086c8bec)

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)
master

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_complex (id INT, name VARCHAR(50), created_at TIMESTAMP) PARTITION BY RANGE (id) (PARTITION p1 VALUES LESS THAN (100), PARTITION p2 VALUES LESS THAN (200), PARTITION p3 VALUES LESS THAN (300));
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_name ON test_complex (name);
INSERT INTO test_complex VALUES (1, 'Alice', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (2, 'Bob', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP), (150, 'Charlie', CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
ALTER TABLE test_complex DROP PARTITION p1, p2;
key_bug_features: drop multiple partitions with global index
